<!-- generated by scripts/generate.sh (catalog step) from .claude-plugin/marketplace.json descriptions + plugins/*/.claude-plugin/plugin.json keywords — do not edit this file -->

# Marketplace plugin catalog

One row per marketplace plugin: `name — [keywords] — description`. Regenerated by scripts/generate.sh; consumed by the plugin-scout skill.

a11y — [ui-ux, frontend, review] — Dedicated accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA audit for markup, contrast, keyboard navigation, focus management, forms, media, and ARIA — one line per violation with the concrete fix — plus an a11y-engineer worker that applies the fix list, preferring native semantics over ARIA patches and tagging each change with its WCAG criterion. Includes /a11y:audit.
adspower — [navigator, automation] — AdsPower Local API navigator: current endpoints from live docs, the profile lifecycle (list/create profile, start/stop browser, obtain the CDP/WebSocket endpoint), rate limits and status codes, and handing the started browser to Playwright or Puppeteer. Includes /adspower:check and an AdsPower-keyword reminder hook. Defers general third-party doc verification to api-docs-first.
api-auth — [api, security, backend] — API authentication and authorization authoring: token models (session, opaque, JWT), Laravel Sanctum vs Passport, OAuth2/OIDC flows with Authorization Code + PKCE, scopes and least privilege, refresh-token rotation with reuse detection, secure httpOnly-cookie storage, hashing at rest, and revocation/expiry — a firing api-auth skill and a /api-auth:review command. The day-one token-secured-API decisions and top OWASP auth failures.
api-design — [api, backend, review] — REST API design: resource naming, status-code discipline, pagination/filtering/sorting conventions, versioning strategy, RFC 9457 error format, idempotency, Laravel API Resources. Includes /api-design:review and /api-design:scaffold (spec-first: OpenAPI → Laravel routes/FormRequests/Resources). For designing your own APIs; defers consuming third-party API docs to api-docs-first and Meta/Facebook platform specifics to meta-api.
api-docs-first — [api, docs] — Verify current API docs before writing integration code; ask for a URL or file when docs are missing. For consuming third-party APIs; defers designing your own REST APIs to api-design and Meta/Facebook platform specifics to meta-api.
approaches — [process, planning] — Solution-approach deliberation: before non-trivial implementation, generate 2-3 structurally different approaches, compare trade-offs, pick with a stated kill-trigger — plus a strategy catalog (tracer bullet, walking skeleton, spike, strangler fig, inversion, Polya) mapped to the risk each one beats, and an auto-nudged opinion round where four parallel blind opinion-lens subagents (Standards Purist, Quality-over-Speed, Pragmatist-Minimalist, Skeptic-Investigator) argue refactor-shaped tasks and synthesis converges to one pick in a single round. Includes /approaches:compare and /approaches:opinions.
automation-builder — [automation, planning, worker] — Browser-automation planner and worker: a think-process skill that turns an automation goal into a tool choice (Playwright/Puppeteer vs anti-detect AdsPower/Kameleo/Camoufox) and a sequenced plan, plus a browser-automation-engineer agent that scaffolds and runs the automation. Includes /automation-builder:build and an automation-keyword reminder hook. Pairs with the playwright, puppeteer, adspower, kameleo, and camoufox plugins; defers doc verification to api-docs-first.
automations-suite — [suite, automation] — Meta-bundle: the browser-automation category in one install — Playwright, Puppeteer, AdsPower, Kameleo, Camoufox navigators plus the automation-builder planner and browser-automation-engineer agent. Uninstall cleanly with /automations-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.
brain — [tooling, hooks] — A committed, Obsidian-style codebase map for a project: brain/INDEX.md indexes areas, key files, and notable classes/entrypoints so a fresh Claude session starts oriented. A SessionStart hook injects the compact map (with a staleness hint when it lags HEAD); /brain reads it and /brain index builds/refreshes it. Map-only tracer (Phase 1a); an anchored-notes layer is planned. Nothing outside brain/ is ever modified.
build-vs-buy — [planning, process, hooks] — Gate zero before writing generic capability: check whether a library, service, or stdlib feature already solves it — candidate health table, take/wrap/write verdict, never-hand-roll list (crypto, auth, timezones, parsers). A UserPromptSubmit hook nudges the check when a prompt proposes building a commonly-solved capability. Includes /build-vs-buy:check.
camoufox — [navigator, automation] — Camoufox navigator: current Python usage from live docs (camoufox.com), launch options (humanize, geoip, os, proxy, block_images, config), the Playwright-Firefox integration it exposes, and fingerprint injection. Includes /camoufox:check and a Camoufox-keyword reminder hook. Defers general third-party doc verification to api-docs-first.
claude-authoring — [tooling] — Authoring guides for Claude Code artifacts — skills, agents, commands, hooks, plugins — plus a routine-detector that suggests scaffolding a project skill when work turns repetitive, a project-skill-suggester that proactively offers one when a task's cards share uncovered repository-specific knowledge, and /claude-authoring:new-skill|new-agent|new-command|new-hook|new-plugin scaffold commands.
code-architecture — [process, quality] — Engineering process: plan-before-code, YAGNI, SOLID principles applied with judgment, task orchestration, work verification, low-cognitive-load code, KISS/DRY simplicity principles. Owns code-level structure — units, interfaces, file placement; defers system-level topology (service boundaries, scaling, caching) to the system-design plugin.
code-review — [review, quality] — Stack-agnostic code review: correctness bugs, code smells, convention drift on any diff or PR — severity-sorted one-line findings. Defers structure/YAGNI to code-architecture, security depth to security, stack idioms to the per-framework review plugins. Includes /code-review:review, a code-reviewer agent, and a code-smells skill.
compaction-advisor — [hooks, tooling] — Advice-only nudge to run /compact when a session grows long. A UserPromptSubmit hook counts user turns per session and, on a repeating 50-turn interval, prints one line suggesting /compact if an early chunk is no longer relevant to the current task — the model judges relevance from the nudge text. Resets on SessionStart source=compact|clear so it never nags right after a compaction or a context clear. Single self-healing state file per working tree, warn-only and fail-open: any error exits 0 silently, a corrupt or foreign-session state file re-seeds rather than going silent. No token API, no byte-size thresholds, and it never runs /compact itself — the user decides.
concurrency — [backend, review] — Application-level concurrency safety: check-then-act races, optimistic vs pessimistic locking, idempotency keys for retried operations, queue-consumer dedup under at-least-once delivery, distributed locks with TTL and fencing, async parallel-write pitfalls — safe when two copies run at once. Includes /concurrency:review.
data-privacy — [security, governance, review] — Regulatory data-handling (GDPR/CCPA): PII classification and data mapping, data-subject rights (access, erasure, portability) with reach across logs/caches/backups/processors, consent and lawful basis, retention and deletion policies, minimization, pseudonymization, and audit trails — a firing data-privacy skill and a /data-privacy:review command. The compliance layer above code-level security review.
database — [database, review, worker] — Database design and implementation: engine-agnostic schema, migration (expand→contract), indexing, query-shape, and connection-pool discipline — a firing database-design skill, a /database:review command, a database-engineer worker, and a PreToolUse guard that asks for confirmation before a destructive statement (DROP/TRUNCATE/unqualified DELETE-UPDATE) lands. Defers dialect statement audits to the sql/mysql/mariadb/postgresql plugins.
db-suite — [suite, database] — Meta-bundle: the database category in one install — engine-agnostic SQL discipline, MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL best practices, and the database worker agent. Uninstall cleanly with /db-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.
debugging — [process, quality] — Systematic debugging: root cause before any fix — reproduce first, read the actual error, isolate with hypothesis tests and bisection, one change at a time, verify against the original symptom; escalation rule after three failed fixes. Ships a delegatable debugger agent for handing off the long reproduce-bisect investigation. Includes /debugging:debug.
decision-records — [docs, process, governance] — Architecture Decision Records: persist every significant technical choice — approach picks, schema shapes, dependency adoptions — to docs/adr/ with context, rejected options, consequences, and a revisit-when trigger. Status lifecycle proposed/accepted/superseded. Includes /decision-records:new.
design-patterns — [process, quality] — Design pattern selection across the full GoF catalog: which pattern fits where, when NOT to use one, and look-alike disambiguation.
design-preview — [ui-ux, frontend] — Real-component visual decisions for Vite + React projects: renders 2-3 candidate variants with the project's OWN components on its own dev server via a scratch HTML entry (zero edits to existing files), behind a strict consent gate with guaranteed cleanup. Escalation tier above static shell mockups — falls back to them when Vite is absent. Includes /design-preview:preview.
dev-env — [devops, tooling] — Dev environment initiator: scan composer/npm manifests, lockfiles, .env DSNs, and CI images, then generate a docker-compose.yml and Dockerfile matched to the actual stack — PHP version and extensions, Node, database engine, Redis, queues, mail. Includes /dev-env:init to generate and /dev-env:review to audit existing docker files. Pairs with stack-scan. Owns local development environments; defers CI/CD pipelines and production deploys to the devops plugin.
devops — [devops, pipeline, review, worker] — DevOps pipeline and infrastructure: CI/CD ordering, container/image hygiene, Kubernetes resource limits and probes, deploy strategy with rollback, and secrets handling — a firing devops-practices skill, a /devops:review command, and a devops-engineer worker + devops-reviewer pair. Owns infra-layer observability wiring (defers in-code instrumentation to observability); defers local docker-compose dev environments to dev-env.
docs-upkeep — [docs, process] — Documentation drift prevention: after any change, sync README claims, changelog entries, ADR links, and API docs with what the code now actually does — docs updated in the same change that invalidated them. Includes /docs-upkeep:check.
error-handling — [generic, review] — Language-agnostic error-handling discipline: crash on programmer errors, handle operational errors where you can act, typed errors over message-string matching, cause chains preserved across boundaries, one report per failure — no swallowed exceptions, no catch-log-continue towers. Includes /error-handling:review.
estimation — [planning, process] — Task sizing and effort estimation: S/M/L/XL classes with concrete anchors, reference-class calibration against work actually completed, uncertainty multipliers for unknowns, split triggers for oversized work, and estimate-vs-actual tracking persisted to a durable append-only ledger (with deferred-work parking) so calibration accumulates across runs. Includes /estimation:size.
event-driven — [backend, review] — Message-driven architecture discipline: broker/transport choice, topic and partition design, event schema versioning, delivery semantics (assume at-least-once), consumer idempotency, the outbox pattern for atomic state-change-and-publish, sagas with compensation, and dead-letter handling — a firing event-driven skill and a /event-driven:review command. Cross-broker; defers in-process framework queues to the stack plugin.
everything — [suite] — Meta-bundle: installs every plugin in this marketplace as a dependency — all stacks, all workflows, all agents. One install, full suite. Uninstall cleanly with /everything:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.
frontend-suite — [suite, frontend] — Meta-bundle: the frontend category in one install — UI/UX stacks, React, React Native, Vue 3, JavaScript, TypeScript, Vite, Inertia, Livewire, the generalist web worker, real-component visual decisions (design-preview), greenfield shadcn staging (shadcn-studio), and accessibility auditing. Uninstall cleanly with /frontend-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.
git-workflow — [git, process] — Git workflow discipline: isolated worktrees for feature work, a structured finish protocol for development branches (verify, present options, merge/PR/park, clean up), and review-exchange rigor — what to include when requesting review, and technical verification before implementing received feedback. Includes /git-workflow:finish.
graphql-grpc — [api, backend, review] — GraphQL and gRPC API discipline: GraphQL schema design, the N+1/DataLoader problem, per-field resolver authorization, query depth/complexity limits, cursor pagination and error shaping; gRPC protobuf evolution (field-number safety), streaming, deadlines, and canonical status codes — a firing graphql-grpc skill and a /graphql-grpc:review command. Distinct paradigms from REST; defers REST to api-design.
hindsight — [process, tooling] — Cross-session self-improvement loop: mines session transcripts for recurring friction and proposes CLAUDE.md rules, skill/plugin ideas, and failed-approach warnings — applied only on approval.
i18n — [frontend, review] — Internationalization and localization: semantic translation keys and catalog hygiene, ICU MessageFormat pluralization and gender, locale-aware dates/numbers/currency via Intl, RTL layout with logical properties, fallback chains, and tooling-driven extraction — a firing i18n skill and a /i18n:review command. For products shipping outside one language; skips a genuinely single-locale app.
inertia — [frontend, backend, review] — Inertia.js best practices (Laravel + Vue/React/Svelte adapters): partial reloads, deferred and lazy props, useForm flow, shared data via middleware, prop hygiene, SSR, code splitting, v1 vs v2 and adapter awareness. Includes /inertia:review.
intent-guard — [governance, hooks, review] — Lean, warn-only drift and corner-cutting guard for a coding agent. Anchors one declared intent X (with criteria) per session, injects a single continuous UserPromptSubmit reminder to review your diff before you call work done, and drives a drift-review diff-review-at-done against the session-base commit — an independent reviewer subagent when running top-level, honest self-review inside a subagent. No completion-blocking hook and no per-action ceremony; every output is advisory. Fills the mid-run intent-vs-action seam between coverage-check (entry) and work-verification (exit). Three warn-only hooks (UserPromptSubmit capture, PostToolUse note, Stop summary) plus the drift-review skill. Includes /intent-guard:intent and /intent-guard:status. Not tamper-proof and not a security boundary — cooperative and defeatable by an agent with shell access; it raises the visibility cost of casual drift, not an adversary.
javascript — [frontend, review] — Vanilla JavaScript best practices: version-aware ES feature floors from engines/browserslist/lockfile, strict equality and coercion traps, ESM vs CommonJS interop, async correctness and the event loop, this-binding and closures, immutability, error handling, boundary validation, number precision and BigInt, security at boundaries. Includes /javascript:review.
kameleo — [navigator, automation] — Kameleo Local API navigator: current endpoints and SDK usage from live docs, the fingerprint → profile → start flow, connecting Playwright/Puppeteer to a started profile over CDP, and profile/fingerprint configuration. Includes /kameleo:check and a Kameleo-keyword reminder hook. Defers general third-party doc verification to api-docs-first.
laravel — [backend, review, worker] — Laravel best practices: Eloquent N+1 prevention, form requests, service layer, queues, policies, a per-version leverage map (Laravel 10/11/12), and mass-assignment security — plus the shared backend-engineer worker (PHP/Laravel) the php/laravel review commands route their fixes to.
livewire — [frontend, backend, review] — Livewire 3 best practices: component conventions, wire:model modifiers, performance, Alpine interop.
llm-app — [backend, review] — LLM application engineering: eval harnesses and regression gates (evaluate, don't eyeball), RAG pipelines (chunking, embeddings, retrieval quality + re-ranking, grounding and citation), prompt versioning, prompt-injection defense, token-cost control, and context management — a firing llm-app skill and a /llm-app:review command. Defers provider API specifics to the claude-api skill.
mariadb — [database, review] — MariaDB 10.6+ best practices: not-MySQL divergences, RETURNING, sequences, system-versioned tables, UUID type, uca1400 collations, JSON-as-text reality, Galera awareness.
meta-api — [api, navigator, docs] — Meta (Facebook) developer platform navigator: always-current Graph API version from the changelog, predefined doc links per product (Graph, Pages, Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger, Marketing), platform conventions — tokens, fields, pagination, errors, webhooks — and required-permissions answers with App Review awareness. Includes /meta-api:check and a Meta-keyword reminder hook. Meta platform only; defers general third-party doc verification to api-docs-first and designing your own APIs to api-design.
mysql — [database, review] — MySQL 8.0+ best practices: InnoDB clustered-PK design, utf8mb4, strict sql_mode, online DDL, gap locks, version leverage 8.0 through 8.4 LTS, EXPLAIN ANALYZE.
observability — [observability, review, worker] — Application observability with judgment: structured JSON logs with correlation IDs, log levels that mean something, RED/USE metrics without cardinality bombs, trace-context propagation, symptom-based alerting, honest health checks — plus an observability-engineer worker that adds the instrumentation (deferring infra-layer wiring to devops). Includes /observability:review.
orchestration — [process, worker] — Subagent orchestration discipline: delegation contracts (self-contained prompts, compressed evidence-backed returns, model/effort tiering, scout-then-fanout, writer isolation), verification panels (refuter voting, judge panels, loop-until-dry, completeness critic), and agent conventions (engineer/reviewer naming taxonomy, one-surface PROACTIVELY arbitration, the shared apply-fixes contract). Includes /orchestration:review.
packages — [tooling, process] — Composer/npm dependency hygiene: semver constraint strategy, lockfile discipline, security-audit triage, and patch/minor/major upgrade lanes. Managing deps already in the project — whether to add one is build-vs-buy's job.
payments — [backend, security, review] — Payments and billing discipline (Stripe/Paddle/Braintree): PCI scope minimization via tokenization, integer-minor-unit money, signature-verified idempotent webhooks, subscription state machines and activation races, dunning and proration, and an append-only ledger with reconciliation — a firing payments skill and a /payments:review command. The failure class is double-charges and revenue leaks.
performance — [performance, review, worker] — Performance tuning: measure-first profiling, N+1/index/payload/bundle/Core Web Vitals hotspots, cache correctness (stampede, TTL, eviction, staleness), and percentile load testing — a firing performance-tuning skill, a /performance:review command, and a worker agent that optimizes only what a measurement proves slow.
php — [backend, review] — PHP best practices: strict types, === discipline, PSR conventions, version-aware 8.1-8.5 leverage map pinned to the composer.json floor, exceptions, DateTimeImmutable, boundary security, static analysis.
php-suite — [suite, backend] — Meta-bundle: the PHP category in one install — PHP and Laravel best practices, Livewire, Inertia, Vite (Laravel's default asset bundler), and the generalist web worker. Uninstall cleanly with /php-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.
playwright — [navigator, automation] — Playwright automation navigator: resolves the current API from live docs (playwright.dev), link map per area (locators, auto-waiting, network interception, storageState auth, test runner, trace viewer, connectOverCDP), robust-automation patterns, and driving an anti-detect browser (AdsPower/Kameleo) over CDP. Includes /playwright:check and a Playwright-keyword reminder hook. Defers general third-party doc verification to api-docs-first.
plugin-scout — [tooling] — Scan the current project's manifests and suggest which cc-plugins-marketplace plugins to install — stack-matched tier and always-useful tier — then install the picked ones after confirmation. Includes /plugin-scout:suggest.
postgresql — [database, review] — PostgreSQL 14+ best practices: MVCC and vacuum reality, rich types (timestamptz, jsonb, uuid), index arsenal (partial, expression, GIN, BRIN, CONCURRENTLY), lock-aware migrations, ON CONFLICT/RETURNING, version leverage through 17/18.
process-suite — [suite, process] — Meta-bundle: the engineering-process category in one install — git workflow, approach deliberation, decision records, retrospectives, hindsight mining, build-vs-buy gates, rollout planning, docs upkeep, estimation, subagent orchestration, task execution, stack scanning, and plugin scouting. Uninstall cleanly with /process-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.
puppeteer — [navigator, automation] — Puppeteer automation navigator: current API from live docs (pptr.dev), link map (page API, waitForSelector, request interception, puppeteer.connect over CDP, puppeteer-extra stealth), robust-automation patterns, and attaching to an anti-detect browser (AdsPower/Kameleo) via browserWSEndpoint. Includes /puppeteer:check and a Puppeteer-keyword reminder hook. Defers general third-party doc verification to api-docs-first.
quality-suite — [suite, quality] — Meta-bundle: the code-quality category in one install — review, architecture principles, design patterns, testing, security, accessibility, debugging, performance, resilience, dependency hygiene, observability, error handling, and concurrency safety. Uninstall cleanly with /quality-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.
react — [frontend, review] — React best practices: hooks rules, render/memo performance, state management, component patterns — plus a react-server-state skill for server-cache libraries (TanStack Query/SWR/RTK Query), cache keys and invalidation, mutations and optimistic updates, keeping server data out of useState and avoiding refetch storms.
react-native — [frontend, review] — React Native best practices: list performance, navigation, platform-specific code, animations.
resilience — [backend, review] — Failure-mode design for integration points: timeouts everywhere, retries with backoff and idempotency, circuit breaking, graceful degradation, backpressure, delivery semantics — designed in, not bolted on after the outage. Includes /resilience:review.
retrospective — [process] — Post-milestone learning loop: five-minute retro after a task run or feature lands — surprises become CLAUDE.md candidates, repetition becomes skill suggestions, friction becomes process tweaks. Closes the amnesia gap where every run starts smart and ends forgotten. Includes /retrospective:run.
reuse-guard — [governance, hooks] — Warn-only reuse-hygiene guard so a cooperating agent does not build on dead code. Two-tier by design. Tier 1 — language-agnostic ambient reuse-a-corpse: a PostToolUse hook greps incoming edits for references to symbols in a session-cached deprecated-symbol set (union of @deprecated / #[deprecated] / [Obsolete] / DEPRECATED-style markers) and warns once on a hit, silent otherwise. Tier 2 — on-demand /reuse-guard:check [symbol|path]: per-language dead-code tool shellout plus export-aware orphan / reachability detection and a full deprecated-reference report. Not tamper-proof and not a security boundary — a heuristic string match, warn-only, and defeatable by anyone with shell access; it raises the visibility of casual reuse-of-dead-code, not an adversary. Precision lives in the Tier-2 command, not the ambient hook.
rollout — [planning, process] — Per-feature rollout planning: feature flags, backward compatibility, staged exposure (internal → percentage → full), migration sequencing (expand-migrate-contract), and a rollback path stated BEFORE ship. Includes /rollout:plan.
secret-scanning — [security, hooks] — Secret-leak prevention: a PreToolUse hook that blocks a Write/Edit introducing a high-confidence secret (cloud keys, private-key blocks, provider tokens, assigned secret literals) before it reaches disk, plus a /secret-scanning:scan command to sweep a path or diff on demand. Fail-open, high-confidence-only, with a fixture escape.
security — [security, review, worker] — Security for web apps: OWASP-aligned code review (injection, XSS, CSRF, authz vs authn, mass assignment, uploads, secrets, dependency audit) mapped to PHP/Laravel and JS/Vue, PLUS a design-phase threat-modeling skill (STRIDE, trust boundaries, abuse cases, dispositions). Includes /security:review and a security-engineer worker agent.
shadcn-studio — [ui-ux, frontend, tooling] — Greenfield interactive shadcn staging: stands up a self-contained shadcn + Vite (Tailwind v4) sandbox on its own dev server and renders agent-authored component variants side by side — real interactivity (sort/filter/dialog), not static HTML. For idea-stage / non-React work where design-preview cannot run (no host Vite+React app). Consent-gated, scratch outside the work tree, verified cleanup, static-shell fallback. Includes /shadcn-studio:stage.
skill-router — [tooling, hooks] — File-aware skill auto-routing: a PostToolUse hook injects a directive to load the relevant best-practice skill(s) when you edit a matching file (PHP/Laravel/Blade, TS/JS, React/Vue/Inertia/Livewire, SQL, components, tests, Dockerfiles, OpenAPI), a SessionStart hook primes a repo-tailored skill index, and low-confidence content signals surface in a SessionEnd digest. Every relevant skill co-fires per edit (a11y alongside ui-ux); fail-open, once per skill per session.
sql — [database, review] — Engine-agnostic SQL best practices: sargable predicates, join correctness, indexing logic, NULL three-valued logic, transactions, constraints, keyset pagination, safe migrations, parameterization.
stack-scan — [tooling] — Inventory the project's actual stack: manifests and lockfiles (composer, npm/yarn/pnpm/bun), runtimes, engine constraints, docker images. Reports required vs installed versions and flags drift, missing locks, and EOL majors.
system-design — [process, review, worker] — System-level design: service boundaries on data ownership, scaling paths, cache placement, sync vs async integration and its failure modes, single points of failure, plus domain modeling (bounded contexts, aggregates, ubiquitous language) — firing system-design and domain-modeling skills, a /system-design:review command, and a system-architect worker + system-design-reviewer pair. Complements code-architecture (code-level structure) without overlapping it.
task-runner — [worker, process, generic] — Disciplined task execution: one task at a time, scope locked to the task, bounded verify-fix inner loop per task, no drift, no unbounded outer loop. Completion means every check passed with evidence. Ships the shared task-executor agent — the delegatable 'apply the fixes' sink every review routes to — and a PostToolUse scope-lock tripwire that flags edits outside the current task's declared files. Includes parallel-planning: computed subagents-vs-inline verdict with dependency levels, agent count, and speedup estimate via /task-runner:plan.
taskmaster — [planning, process] — Idea-to-execution clarification: brainstorm fuzzy ideas into approved designs, grill requirements until zero ambiguity, deliberate the implementation approach with a blind four-persona round, decide visual choices with mockups, validate whole experiences with an interactive clickable demo, then emit milestone-grouped single-prompt task cards. Outputs live under taskmaster-docs/ (no collision with a project's own docs/).
taskmaster-suite — [suite, planning] — Meta-bundle: the full clarification-to-execution workflow plus every stack-agnostic capability — taskmaster, task-runner, stack-scan, code-architecture, general engineering discipline, UI/UX, and all worker agents. No framework- or dialect-specific plugins. Uninstall cleanly with /taskmaster-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.
testing — [testing, review, worker] — Testing best practices: test pyramid and what to actually test, Pest/PHPUnit and Vitest/Jest idioms, Playwright/Dusk e2e discipline, factories and fixtures, mocking boundaries, flaky-test causes, coverage traps — plus the TDD workflow (red-green-refactor, red-green regression proof for bug fixes). Includes /testing:review and a test-engineer worker agent.
typescript — [frontend, review] — TypeScript best practices: strict mode as the floor, any vs unknown discipline, narrowing over assertions, generics restraint, satisfies, runtime validation at boundaries, tsconfig hygiene. Includes /typescript:review.
ui-ux — [ui-ux, frontend, review, worker] — UI/UX best practices with per-stack skills: shadcn/ui, ReUI, Aceternity UI, Tailwind, CSS3, Bootstrap, CSS Grid, Flexbox — plus a design-tokens skill (spacing/type/radius/elevation/motion scales + semantic color tiering), shadcn theme creation with a live colour preview, a /ui-ux:build entry point, and ui-ux-reviewer + ui-ux-engineer agents.
ultra-deep-research — [research] — Deep-research harness that fans out parallel web searches, tiers sources by provenance, extracts date-stamped claims, and adversarially refutes them before synthesizing a cited report with a contradiction ledger and per-section confidence. Trigger 'ultra-deep-research' escalates to a Workflow-driven loop-until-dry fan-out with multi-vote refutation panels; standard depth runs a portable parallel-Agent fan-out. Ships /ultra-deep-research:research plus researcher and verifier subagents. Writes the report inline and to research/<topic>-<date>.md.
vite — [frontend, review] — Vite best practices: VITE_-prefix env security, dep pre-bundling, code splitting and manualChunks, base for sub-path deploys, dev server.proxy, define stringify pitfalls, import.meta.glob, asset handling, build.target alignment, SSR, library mode, plugin order, HMR guards, version-aware against the locked vite version and vite.config. Includes /vite:review.
vue2 — [frontend, review] — Vue 2.7 best practices: Composition API backport, reactivity caveats, migration readiness. Vue 2 is EOL (Dec 2023) — for maintaining legacy apps only; new work should use vue3.
vue3 — [frontend, review] — Vue 3 best practices: script setup, composables, ref/reactive pitfalls, Pinia.
web-dev — [frontend, backend, worker, review] — Generalist web development pair: a web-developer worker (routing, REST/API integration, forms, state, SSR/CSR) and a frontend-reviewer that audits React/Vue/Inertia/Livewire/TS + react-native/vite against the matching per-framework skill. Stack-agnostic; defers stack idioms to the per-framework plugins.
